front: Composer: Italian Baroque 'Red Priest'; taught at the Ospedale della Pieta orphanage in Venice; over 500 violin concertos catalogued in the Ryom-Verzeichnis (RV) by Peter Ryom

Concerto Collection: four programmatic violin concertos (Spring, Summer, Autumn, Winter), each with a sonnet; depicts buzzing flies, barking dogs, thunderstorms, cuckoos, shepherd's dogs — first four concertos of The Contest Between Harmony and Invention

Composer: wrote The Four Seasons (in The Contest Between Harmony and Invention, Op. 8); also wrote L'estro armonico Op. 3 — transcribed for keyboard by Bach after Johann Ernst of Saxe-Weimar brought it to Weimar

Concerto Collection (Vivaldi): twelve string concertos; Bach transcribed several, including one that became the Concerto for Four Harpsichords (from the 10th concerto); Johann Ernst brought it from Venice to Weimar

Choral Work (Vivaldi): most popular sacred work; rediscovered in Alfredo Casella's 1939 'Vivaldi Week'; opens in D major; ends with 'Cum Sancto Spiritu' fugue; 6th movement 'Domine deus, rex coelestis' for soprano, continuo, oboe/violin
Oratorio (Vivaldi): only surviving oratorio; all roles sung by women; allegorizes Schulenburg's military victory at Corfu; alto sings Holofernes
Composer: name used in partial anagram 'Aldiviva Licante' in Benedetto Marcello's satire Il teatro alla moda; Fritz Kreisler falsely attributed a C major violin concerto to him

Concerto (Vivaldi): Lute Concerto in D major (RV 93) — main theme: descending F-sharp, E, long D; often played today on guitar; Mandolin Concerto in C major (RV 425) frequently paired with Four Seasons
work: Mandolin Concerto in C major / Lute Concerto in D major
